close_reason: Added Supported algorithms table to module-level rustdoc
description:
Location: secretx-pkcs11/README.md and secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs (top-of-crate doc).

What is unclear, and for whom: a downstream user evaluating whether secretx-pkcs11 supports their HSM key cannot tell from README + rustdoc which algorithms work. README line 5 says "compatible with any PKCS#11 v2.40-compliant token" which is an availability claim, not an algorithm claim. The actual matrix:

  - EC P-256 + SHA-256: yes
  - EC P-384, P-521: no (returns "unsupported EC curve" error)
  - Ed25519 / EdDSA: no (returns "unsupported key type" error)
  - RSA 2048 PSS + SHA-256, MGF1-SHA-256, salt 32: yes
  - RSA other key sizes / hash combos / PKCS1v1.5: no
  - DATA objects (CKO_DATA): yes for read/write
  - Certificates (CKO_CERTIFICATE): no
  - Public-key extraction: requires CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO populated by the token

Users will discover the gaps at runtime by hitting the error path, then file an issue.

What would make it clear: a "Supported algorithms" section in the README with the matrix above, and a "Known limitations" section that lists the issues from this review (P-384/P-521 not yet implemented, Ed25519 not yet implemented, CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO required, single-process C_Initialize).

Cross-references: relates to crate-secretsx-nde2.9 (Ed25519), .10 (P-384/P-521), .11 (CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO).
title: README lacks 'Supported algorithms' matrix; users discover gaps at runtime
